# Lecture 5Noise

# Noise

![](img/lec-05-noise_0.png)

![](img/lec-05-noise_1.png)

![](img/lec-05-noise_2.png)

![](img/lec-05-noise_3.png)

Source: EE315a\, B\. Murmann\, Stanford Univ\.

# LTI System

Parseval’s Theorem

Wiener\-Khintchine Theorem \(autocorrelation\)

Linear mean value

Wiener\-Lee relation \(autocorrelation\)

# Total Integrated Noise

From PSD to Tot Int Noise

Signal\-to\-Noise ratio

# RC Lowpass Filter

Source: EE315a\, B\. Murmann\, Stanford Univ\.

# Total Integrated Noise

Source: EE315a\, B\. Murmann\, Stanford Univ\.

# LC Lowpass Filter

Source: EE315a\, B\. Murmann\, Stanford Univ\.

# Total Integrated Noise

Source: EE315a\, B\. Murmann\, Stanford Univ\.

# The Nyquist Theorem

![](img/lec-05-noise_7.png)

Christian C\. Enz and Eric A\. Vittoz\, Charge\-Based MOS Transistor Modeling: The EKV Model for Low\-Power and RF IC Design\, Wiley\, 2006 \(p\. 106\)

Source: EE315a\, B\. Murmann\, Stanford Univ\.

# The Bode Theorem

![](img/lec-05-noise_8.png)

Christian C\. Enz and Eric A\. Vittoz\, Charge\-Based MOS Transistor Modeling: The EKV Model for Low\-Power and RF IC Design\, Wiley\, 2006 \(p\. 107\)

Let’s you calculate the total integrated noise of passive networks without sweating through integrals\!

Source: EE315a\, B\. Murmann\, Stanford Univ\.

# Useful Integrals

A\. Dastgheib and B\. Murmann\, "Calculation of total integrated noise in analog circuits\,"  _IEEE Trans\. on Circuits and Systems I\, _ Vol\. 55\, pp\. 2988\-2993\, Nov\. 2008\.

Source: EE315a\, B\. Murmann\, Stanford Univ\.

# Summary



* The total integrated thermal noise of filter circuits is related to capacitor size
  * Usually a multiple of kT/C
* In filters\, noise is proportional to the filter order\, QP\, and strongly dependent on the implementation\.
* Amplifiers can contribute significantly to \(if not dominate\) the overall filter noise:
  * Minimizing the amplifier noise contribution costs power\,which needs small Rnoise\, i\.e\. large gm \(IBIAS\)\.


Source: EE315a\, B\. Murmann\, Stanford Univ\.
